text: This is a post-test inspection of this reactor.
Now we disassemble the reactor and check the condition of each component after operation.
First, we will inspect the anode side.
You can find there is no obvious liquid accumulation or any flooding.
And the surface appears very dry after the test.
Oh, this is the anode side. This is the anode catalytic. Very, very dry.
Now let's inspect the membrane region.
Here we can see the membrane after test.
This shows that the pre-added 1-molecule H droplet is not efficient to maintain the long-term hydration during the test.
Yeah, also the cathode side. The cathode side is also very dry.
So, the main failure mode of this reactor is still dry out.
Compared to the last one, pre-adding a fuel droplet molecule may improve the initial hydration,
but this is only temporary water and ion supply during operation.
The water can be absorbed, evaporated, or redistributed, but the membrane of PTFE can still lose hydration.
